comment: Catalog-derived Claude model ladder, ordered cheapest to most expensive. `pairs` is the flat model|effort sequence the boundary-search algorithm walks — mirrors Codex's model_registry.py flattening (each model row x its supported efforts, in registry order). Update model names/order here when Anthropic ships new tiers; the skill logic never hardcodes model names.

rungs.good_for: mechanical edits, renames, formatting, boilerplate, simple lookups, small bounded scripts
standard coding, refactors, tests, single-module features
complex multi-file design, hard debugging, architecture
hardest reasoning, ambiguous specs, final repair rung

cheap_first.eligible: bounded text/code tasks where failure is cheap to detect (tests exist, output is verifiable)

skip_routing: exact read-only lookups (answer inline, no delegation)
verification/review of another agent's output (use a different, equal-or-stronger model, never the failed pair)
